skill.name: skill-installer
skill.description: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from curated OpenAI lists or GitHub repository paths.
skill.summary: List and install Codex skills from curated sources or GitHub repositories.
skill.author: openai

security_audit.summary: This official skill uses network access, GitHub tokens, git commands, and filesystem writes for its stated installer purpose. The reviewed scripts validate GitHub paths, check archive extraction paths, and avoid overwriting existing skill directories. No prompt injection attempts or malicious behaviors were identified.

content.value_statement: Installing AI skills manually can be slow and error-prone. This skill lists curated options and installs selected skills from GitHub into Codex.
actual_capabilities: Lists curated skills from the OpenAI skills repository.
Marks skills that are already installed in the local Codex skills directory.
Installs one or more skills from a GitHub repository path.
Supports public downloads and git sparse checkout fallback.
Uses optional GitHub token environment variables for private repositories.
Prevents overwriting an existing destination skill directory.
limitations: Only GitHub sources are supported by the installer scripts.
Network access is required for listing and installation.
Installed skills require a Codex restart before use.
The installer validates SKILL.md presence but does not deeply audit installed skill behavior.

use_cases.title: Add approved skills
use_cases.description: List curated skills and install selected options into the local Codex skills folder.

use_cases.title: Load private team skills
use_cases.description: Install skills from a private GitHub repository using existing git credentials or a token.

use_cases.title: Prepare shared environments
use_cases.description: Install multiple skills from repository paths when setting up a repeatable Codex workspace.

prompt_templates.title: List available skills
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user wants to browse curated options.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill installer to list curated Codex skills and show which ones are already installed.

prompt_templates.title: Install one curated skill
prompt_templates.scenario: User already knows the skill name.
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the curated Codex skill named [skill name], then tell me what changed and whether I need to restart Codex.

prompt_templates.title: Install from GitHub
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a repository path for a skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the Codex skill from GitHub repository [owner/repo] at path [path/to/skill] using ref [branch or tag].

prompt_templates.title: Install multiple private skills
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user is preparing a team environment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Install these Codex skills from private repository [owner/repo] using existing credentials: [path one], [path two], [path three].

output_examples.input: List curated skills I can install.
output: Shows a numbered list of curated skills from the configured repository.
Adds an installed marker next to skills already present locally.
Asks which skills should be installed.

output_examples.input: Install a skill from a GitHub path.
output: Downloads or checks out the requested repository path.
Copies the skill into the local Codex skills directory.
Reports the installed skill name and reminds the user to restart Codex.

best_practices: Install from trusted repositories and reviewed refs when possible.
Restart Codex after installation so new skills are loaded.
Use GitHub tokens only when needed for private repository access.
anti_patterns: Installing unreviewed skills from unknown repositories.
Using a broad token when read-only repository access is enough.
Expecting the installer to audit a downloaded skill automatically.

faq.question: Where are skills installed?
faq.answer: Skills are installed into $CODEX_HOME/skills. If CODEX_HOME is not set, the default is ~/.codex/skills.

faq.question: Can it install from private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use existing git credentials or optional GITHUB_TOKEN and GH_TOKEN environment variables.

faq.question: Does it overwrite existing skills?
faq.answer: No. The installer stops when the destination skill directory already exists.

faq.question: Does it work without network access?
faq.answer: No. Listing and installation require access to GitHub.

faq.question: Can it install more than one skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Multiple repository paths can be provided in one installer run.

faq.question: Does Codex use the skill immediately?
faq.answer: No. Codex must be restarted before newly installed skills are available.
